evals.name: basic-text-search
evals.prompt: Create a Vespa schema for a 'article' document type with fields: title (string, searchable), body (string, searchable with BM25 support), author (string, filterable), published_date (long, sortable), and url (string, stored only). Include a default fieldset combining title and body, and a basic BM25 rank profile.
evals.expected_output: A valid .sd schema file with correct field types, appropriate indexing pipelines (index for text fields, attribute for filter/sort), enable-bm25 on fields used in bm25() ranking, a fieldset, and a rank profile using bm25().

evals.name: hybrid-search-embeddings
evals.prompt: I need a Vespa schema called 'product' for hybrid search. It should have: a title and description for text search with BM25, a 768-dimensional float embedding field with HNSW indexing using angular distance, and a hybrid rank profile that combines BM25 text scores with vector closeness in a second phase. The rank profile should accept a query embedding tensor as input.
evals.expected_output: Schema with string fields having enable-bm25, a tensor<float>(x[768]) field with HNSW config, distance-metric angular, and a rank profile with query input tensor, first-phase text scoring, second-phase combining normalize_linear of text and vector scores.

evals.name: fix-broken-schema
evals.expected_output: Fixed schema addressing: (1) schema/document name mismatch (products vs product), (2) match:exact on attribute-only field has no effect, (3) tensor dimension name mismatch (d[384] vs x[384] in query), (4) missing distance-metric on embedding, (5) bm25(title) used without enable-bm25 on title field, (6) missing summary on category field.

evals.name: ecommerce-filtering
evals.prompt: Create a Vespa schema for an e-commerce product catalog. I need: product_id (unique identifier, fast lookup), name and description (full-text searchable), brand (filterable and searchable), price (numeric, filterable and sortable), categories as an array of strings (filterable), in_stock boolean (fast filter), rating (float), and a map of custom attributes (string to string, with filterable keys). Use appropriate attribute modifiers for performance.
evals.expected_output: Schema with product_id using attribute + fast-search, text fields with index, brand with index + attribute + fast-search, price with attribute + fast-search, categories as array<string> with attribute + fast-search, in_stock bool with attribute + fast-search + rank:filter, rating with attribute, map with struct-field indexing.

evals.name: parent-child-reference
evals.prompt: I'm building a Vespa app with two document types: 'merchant' (parent) with fields name, city, and trust_score; and 'offer' (child) with fields title, price, and a reference to the merchant. The offer schema should import the merchant's name and trust_score so I can use them in ranking without joins at query time. Create both schemas.
evals.expected_output: Two .sd files: merchant schema with name/city/trust_score fields (attributes), and offer schema with reference<merchant> field, import field statements for merchant name and trust_score, and possibly a rank profile using the imported fields.
